It would make an ideal first time buy or investment.Safeguarding Your InformationImportant information regarding cybercrime. It has come to our attention that computer hackers are seeking to target property transactions to access funds. As we understand it, they will either hack a phone, tablet, or PC, maybe by sending out a random email that mistakenly gets opened, or even whilst opening a website, which gives them access to that machine. The reason for us mentioning this to you is to alert you that once they have gained access, they can monitor email correspondence, without you knowing, throughout a transaction. Then, at a relevant point, e.g. just before the exchange of contracts, they can send an email to a client mirroring that of their solicitor asking for funds to be transferred to a certain bank account. That money then, of course, disappears. Fortunately, we have not had such an instance but, feel it important to highlight the fact that if you do receive an email requesting funds, it is worth checking with the person concerned by phone that it is genuine and the bank details are correct, before sending money. This could, of course, affect other areas of finance beyond the property. SUMMARY  A beautifully presented two-bedroom apartment with a westerly aspect offering modern, convenient living in the award-winning Battersea Reach riverside development. Offering 753 sq ft of stylish accommodation comprising a spacious reception room with open plan kitchen, master bedroom with en suite shower room, guest bedroom, bathroom and balcony.The development offers comfortable living with a 24-hour concierge service, resident's gym and conference room, secure underground parking, bike storage, CCTV, lift access, well-maintained communal gardens and onsite amenities.Benefits include a private covered balcony, views across to the river Thames, under floor heating, high-quality fixtures and fittings and will appeal to buy-to-let investors, owner occupiers, first time buyers or those looking for a pied-a-terre in London. THE PROPERTY  The apartment is located on the 4th floor and can be accessed via lift or stairs.  HALL  Entry phone system. Wooden floor. Utility cupboard housing the hot water tank, washer/dryer and providing useful storage space.  KITCHEN  Set to the rear of the reception room but separated by a useful breakfast bar, the modern kitchen offers plenty of upper and lower level cabinets, integrated appliances (fridge/freezer, microwave, dishwasher, hob, oven and wine cooler), inset stainless steel sink and a half (with waste disposal), under counter lighting, tiled floor with underfloor heating.  RECEPTION ROOM 25' 8" x 15' 6" (7.82m x 4.72m) The reception room is a generous size with a wall of floor-to-ceiling windows providing plenty of natural light with door opening to the balcony. The room offers ample space for separate dining and living areas. Wooden flooring with under floor heating, recess lights to ceiling. BALCONY 16' 8" x 4' 1" (5.08m x 1.24m) With a westerly aspect the covered balcony runs the full length of the reception room and benefits from the afternoon sun and views across to the communal gardens towards the river. MASTER BEDROOM 12' 0" x 2' 89" (3.66m x 2.87m) The master bedroom has a floor-to-ceiling window offering oblique views to the river Thames, plus useful built-in storage offering hanging and shelving space. Under flooring heating, recessed spotlights to ceiling.  EN SUITE  Comprising an oversized walk-in shower cubicle, wash-hand basin with mirrored cabinet above, cistern enclosed WC, heated ladder style towel rail. Tiled floor with under floor heating.  Guest Bedroom 10' 5" x 9' 4" (3.18m x 2.84m) The guest bedroom is a good size double room with floor-to-ceiling windows with a westerly aspect offering oblique views to the river Thames. Under flooring heating, recessed spotlights to ceiling. BATHROOM  With stylish fixtures and fittings comprising a panel enclosed bath with overhead shower, wash-hand basin with mirrored cabinets above, cistern enclosed WC, heated ladder style towel rail, tiled floor with under flooring heating. BATTERSEA REACH  Battersea Reach is an award-winning St. George development located on the south bank of the river Thames in Wandsworth. The exclusive development offers a 24hr concierge service, secure underground parking, residents only gymnasium 'Gym & Tonic', business centre with private meeting room, bike storage, lift access, plus six acres of landscaped communal gardens and 300 meters of the Thames Path riverside walkway, which offers a cycling and walking path up and down the river Thames. Further onsite facilities include the Cake Boy French patisserie, a Tesco Express, Michaela Mitoi hair and beauty salon, My Health Care Clinic, Cycle Republic, Edible Kitchen café, Lebanese restaurant, Fitness Space gym, Riverside Nursery and the Young`s Waterfront bar and restaurant. The development is within easy reach of a range of independent shops, cafes and restaurants of Old York Road. The Southside shopping centre in Wandsworth offers a large selection of high street shops, a large Waitrose, Sainsbury`s and Cineworld multiplex cinema along with restaurants, cafes and leisure facilities. The tree-lined streets of Parsons Green and Fulham are just across the river plus within easy reach are the boutiques, art galleries and notable dining on the fashionable Kings Road and Sloane Square, Chelsea.The nearest transport is provided by Wandsworth Town mainline station offering speedy services into Victoria (via Clapham Junction) and Waterloo. There is also a good range of bus services providing easy access to Fulham, Chelsea, Victoria and the southern suburbs. An MBNA Thames Clipper river boat service is now in operation at Plantation Wharf pier located just nearby. The service offers a weekday timetable into Blackfriars and Canary Wharf to the east and Putney to the west.

The property is fronted by a spacious sitting room which focuses around a brick feature wall and open fireplace. All of the rooms are accessed by the long, welcoming entrance hallway. Next in line are the bright, elegant bedrooms - the master bedroom features original wood floors and opens onto a courtyard garden. The second bedroom is smaller in size yet caters for a queen sized double bed. Further on, the kitchen/breakfast room has been beautifully designed in a traditional come contemporary style, with ample space for a table.  A unique benefit is the separate utility room that houses a washing machine and tumble dryer. The bathroom is uniquely spacious and, in keeping with the rest of the property, offers a smart finish. A bright, sunny and spacious three to four bedroom semi-detached, sat right in the heart of Hale End's leafy green environs you're surrounded by open green spaces, peaceful streets and excellent schools. To the rear you have an impressive south facing garden and there's heaps of development potential.The open green spaces of Epping Forest, perfect for morning jogs, afternoon picnics and evening strolls, is just a little over five minutes on foot.IF YOU LIVED HEREYou'll be stretching out in more than 1200 square feet of bright, flexible living space, with plenty of room for you and yours to grow. On the ground floor, pride of place is taken by your thirty foot long, dual aspect through lounge, handsomely decked out with blonde engineered hardwood underfoot and striking statement chimney breast, all flooded with natural light.Step into the kitchen and you have another capacious hosting option; 170 square feet with dark hardwood underfoot, an extra-wide stainless steel chef's oven, glossy black splashbacks and plenty of space for dining. Once again, there's no shortage of natural light in here thanks to the large skylight and patio doors leading into the garden. And it's quite a garden.South facing and over fifty feet in length, the part-sheltered patio leads to an immaculate length of lawn, flanked by colourful beds, timber fencing and mature greenery. There's even a handy shed at the bottom. Back inside, and the ground floor is completed by an extra shower room and a semi open-plan second 120 square foot space currently in use as a fourth bedroom but equally suited to a home office or study.Upstairs your master bedroom is another bright and spacious affair, with that lovely oversized bay window, hardwood floors and floor to ceiling storage.  Bedroom two's a generous single while bedroom three is another double with pleasant garden views. Finally, your family bathroom's exquisite, with marbled floors, designer fittings and an L shaped tub with marbled surround.Outside, Highams Park station is less than a half mile on foot, and will get you directly to Liverpool Street in just twenty three minutes. The station's surrounded by something of a social hub, with a good choice of eating drinking establishments just minutes away.WHAT ELSE? - There are sixteen primary/secondary schools just in a one mile radius, all rated 'Good' or better by Ofsted. The 'Outstanding' Oakhill Primary is less than ten minutes walk. - You have off street parking for two cars and drivers can be on the North Circular in less than five minutes. - With the loft space so far undeveloped, you have plenty of scope to extend the property further, potentially adding an entire Dormer extension (subject to the usual permissions).Reception Room - 9.3m x 3.9m (30'6" x 12'9") - Kitchen - 6.4m x 5.1m (20'11" x 16'8") - Shower Room - 2.0m x 1.5m (6'6" x 4'11") - Bedroom/Study - 6.0m x 2.5m (19'8" x 8'2") - Bedroom - 3.4m x 2.0m (11'1" x 6'6") - Bedroom - 4.8m x 3.0m (15'8" x 9'10") - Bedroom - 3.7m x 3.0m (12'1" x 9'10") - Bathroom - 2.5m x 2.2m (8'2" x 7'2") - Garden - 15.24m (50) -

Keating Estates are proud to bring to market a handsome example of a well-proportioned three bedroom Victorian terraced house on the highly sought-after Hargwyne Street, complete with low-maintenance private garden and in great condition throughout.Full Description - Perfectly positioned between the amenities in Brixton, Clapham and Stockwell, this three bedroom Victorian home will appeal to purchasers who are contemplating moving to all three of these sought-after locations. With a varied array of nightlife, restaurants, shops and transport links on the doorstep, purchasers will be spoiled for choice.This freehold house is a lovely mix of characterful features and contemporary design. The ground floor of the property comprises a wonderfully bright double aspect reception room, with a bay window, wooden floor boards, shutters, coving and high ceilings. Towards the rear of the ground floor is a WC plus the modern kitchen with ample space for a dining table, coupled with plenty of worktop and storage space. Glass bi-folding doors allow the living space to flow seamlessly into the well-equipped private garden, a secluded and low-maintenance outdoor area, perfect for hosting summer BBQs or relaxing with a book and enjoying some peace and quiet.On the upper floor sits the three well-proportioned bedrooms and family bathroom. The master bedroom at the front of the house being particularly large, spanning the width of the building with high ceilings and a beautiful bay window allowing for a light and airy atmosphere. The second two double bedrooms are comfortable double rooms with views over the neighbouring gardens at the rear. Nestled between the two rear bedrooms is a sleek family bathroom with a separate bath and walk-in shower. The house has been lovingly cared for and thoughtfully refurbished to a high standard throughout over a number of years by the current owners.Planning permission has been approved to extend into the loft space and create a second storey at the rear of the house. Plans have been drawn to create a forth bedroom with a walk-in wardrobe and an en-suite bathroom with a glass roof. With vast living space, development potential, three well-appointed bedrooms and an abundance of characterful features on top of a charming private garden, it truly is hard to remind yourself that you are indeed still in the heart of Brixton, located on a sought-after, quiet, residential street. This well-presented family home is situated a short stroll from The Ritzy and the excellent array of shops and restaurants in Brixton Village. The location will be perfect for anyone who wants to be close to the action, yet retain a sense of calm: this quiet street feels a world away from the hustle and bustle of Brixton town centre. As far as locations go, it doesn’t get much more central: Brixton station is just a five-minute walk, first stop on the Victoria Line, arguably the best line on the London Underground for fast links into central London and beyond. The restaurants, nightlife and shopping that have made Brixton so popular are all on the doorstep, making this property an ideal purchase for anyone looking to be as close as possible to the amenities on offer. Clapham High Street and Stockwell are both within a ten-minute stroll with access to the Northern Line and the Overground, covering the wants and needs of the majority of purchasers.Freehold.

The Development  This is a unique new community that infuses life into a richly historic site. From the 19th century it was a location for one of Bermondsey's many tanneries, and later, it contained premises owned by Crosse and Blackwell who made their famous Branston Pickle here.  London Square has harnessed the talents of masterplan architects to create a vibrant destination that opens up a site long hidden from view.  A collection of one, two and three bedroom apartments, duplexes and duplexes. Between each of the buildings will be 8 new courtyards and garden squares with walkways connecting the development to the surrounding streets, bringing a new lease of life.  Based in Bermondsey since 1993, Tannery Arts, an independent charity making contemporary art accessible to the public will be an integral element of the development. Creating a double height exhibition space, new studios and display cases along Tannery Way. London Square Bermondsey is also incredibly well located. Bermondsey Street is just a short walk away offering a treasure trove of the quirky and individual. Discover Borough Market one of the most exciting places in the city to buy fine food from all over the world.  If your Monday-to-Friday life takes you into the city both More London and London Bridge are easily reachable by bike or on foot. Canary wharf is just two stops east on the Jubilee line from Bermondsey station which is approximately 14 minutes walk away.  It also has its fair share of open space, with the newly regenerated Bermondsey Spa gardens just around the corner or Southwark Park with 61 acres of green space just a 9 minute cycle away. And it's equally well placed for higher education opportunities.  Images depict the Show Home and CGIs of London Square Bermondsey and are indicative only.

Moments away from Hyde park, this wonderful apartment occupies the first floor of a Georgian townhouse, set on a quaint garden square, abundant with tall trees and lushes greenery. Built in 1826 the townhouse containing this apartment is unmistakably Georgian boasting a charming, historic, exposed brick facade with a white "smooth faced" rusticated base storey. This apartment benefits greatly from the Georgian architecture of the building as it occupies the "Piano Noble" first floor,( which historically served as reception rooms and as such were purpose built with considerably taller ceilings and larger windows than than those of the other floors). This tastefully modernised apartment capitalises on its Piano Noble first floor position by using the additional ceiling height to create a mezzanine containing a bedroom with an en-suite bathroom whilst retaining the ceiling heights and grandeur in the reception room/dining room. The reception/dining room also benefits  from three original, large windows, each with French doors  that open onto a balcony overlooking the beautiful garden square. Under the mezzanine is a kitchen benefiting from modern appliances as well as another bedroom complete with an en-suite bathroom. A graceful floating staircase, accentuated by under step lighting, leads to the mezzanine and adds to the apartments contemporary aesthetic. The mezzanine structure itself is also sympathetically in keeping with the modern interior decor. It seamlessly blends into the reception room as a floor to ceiling decorative wall feature, composed of a light coloured stone, with two elegant strips of artificial shrubbery running down it.Hyde park Square is a fantastic location moments from Hyde Park, situated in a highly desirable part of W2. Neighbouring Connaught Street, residents are provided with a choice of restaurants, coffee shops, clothing boutiques and convenience stores; Oxford Street is also nearby, offering the famous Selfridges store as well as other well-known shops and attractions.The property is excellently placed for transport links including the nearest underground stations, Marble Arch (Central line), Edgware Road (Circle line) and Paddington (Bakerloo, Circle, District, Hammersmith and City Lines); as well as Paddington mainline railway station, providing city, national and international connections via the Heathrow Express. What The Sellers Say -“We both loved the area and had lived here for a few years before we looked into buying a property full of character and history. We were drawn to this converted Victorian Hall until our work commitments led us to move away. We believe it was built in the early 1900’s as a Salvation Army Hall for the people of East Greenwich. It has been a very successful rental property over the years for us and we’ll be sad to say goodbye. The flat has loads to offer, there is scope to develop and reconfigure the upstairs, and, possibly add more dormer space. We will always have amazing memories of living here in Greenwich. In a place so rammed full of culture and history and yet peaceful with river walks and park picnics. Perfect to commute from, close to central London and ideal if you want to explore by train, bike, walk or boat, (the Thames Clipper being one of our favourites!) ”

A stunning dual-aspect loft-style apartment in the historic Colmans Wharf building, with over 1000 sq.ft of internal space, canal-facing private terrace and allocated parking.  Built in 1899, Colmans Wharf is one of six buildings that formed The Spratts dog food factory.  Situated alongside the Limehouse Cut canal, the factory produced and stored a range of goods over many decades until being converted into authentic industrial loft-style units and sold as ‘shell’ in the late 1980s.  This beautiful split-level apartment has been cleverly designed to take full advantage of it’s prime position in the building and 3.2m ceiling height, to provide a highly functional space with masses of storage and natural light, whilst also retaining its flexibility, canal views and many of the original features, such as the crittal windows, exposed brickwork and iron beams. The area around the Limehouse Cut continues to undergo major regeneration, with gyms, cafes and a range of independent shops all opening nearby in the past few years. There are the green spaces of Mile End and Victoria Park nearby and even The Queen Elizabeth Olympic village can reached with a 25 minute walk along the canal towpath. Transport links to the City, West London and Canary Wharf are very good with Langdon Park DLR station 0.2 miles away and Bromley-by- Bow tube station 0.5m away. (District, Circle and Hammersmith & City lines). The property is being sold chain free, in excellent condition and benefits from an allocated secure parking space and a share of the freehold.

description: Comments from the owner:What do you love most about your home? We love the open-plan upstairs area, it gets light from both ends of the house but is also wonderfully cosy in the evenings. It's the perfect space for entertaining, watching movies and sports, cooking, and relaxing. The large bedroom gets loads of light and we use it for an office and music room, and the other bedroom we've used as a calming space for sleeping. The garden has been an absolute joy as well with morning sunshine to enjoy breakfast and coffee in the garden, and when it has been really warm, it's nice in the shade and breeze. The built-in benches and storage allow us to relax and have friends over for bbq's, and I've even worked from home on the table out there many days in the summer!Why are you moving? We honestly can't really believe we're moving, we love this home and area so much. But all our friends are south of the river now, and we've decided to go for a larger home and follow the crowd so we can have our friends and family around more often again.What do you love about the area? Everything! The pubs nearby (Drapers Arms, Smokehouse, Crown, Three Johns), the coffee shops (Southpaw!), the restaurants - everything from high end to cheap eats, the fact that you are only a few steps away from the heart of Barnsbury or the bustle of Chapel Market and centre of Angel, the ability to walk to the City and Oxford Street. Even the corner shop is owned by a lovely family and is like having an extension of your pantry a few feet away.Property Details:With a wealth of period appeal, superbly blended with stylish interiors and bespoke detailing, this beautiful property provides a stunning home in a sought after central location in the heart of Islington. The kitchen area itself comprises a sleek range of matching wall and base units with work surfaces and tiling, incorporating inset sink unit, induction with overhead extractor, double wall-mounted oven, and further integrated appliances.  A wide opening leads through into the breath-taking reception area, with a feature cast iron fireplace and wonderful space for relaxing and entertaining.  Descending to the lower level there are two well-sized double bedrooms, each with inbuilt storage, plus a stylish shower room with a contemporary style suite and elegant tiling. At the rear of the property, there is a charming private patio garden that has also been remodeled to offer a perfect escape and place to unwind after a busy day. The property is enviably located within just a few minutes' walk of Angel, with Kings Cross St Pancras also nearby offering unrivaled cross-London, national and international rail links.  This vibrant location provides a multitude of shops and amenities, with the Regent's Canal also just moments away, and nearby Granary Square offering a further array of fashionable bars, restaurants, and cafes. Ref: ISL

description: Finished to an immaculate standard, this stylish two bedroom apartment on the 9th floor of Java House boasts a bright and airy living space with views of the river and Canary Wharf skyline. Property Details:Accommodation comprises entrance on the raised ground floor with access to a generous double bedroom with bespoke inbuilt mirror fronted cherry wood wardrobes, and access to a modern en-suite shower room.  Stairs lead down to the lower level with access to WC, leading into the bright and spacious reception room with ample space for relaxing and dining, and access out to a delightful split level garden with planted borders and all-weather surface area.  A doorway leads through to the separate kitchen with quiet rear aspect and a modern range of matching units with work surfaces incorporating inset sink unit, gas hob with overhead extractor and electric oven, and further space for appliances.  The property is very conveniently located close to beautiful Clissold Park, and within easy reach of Arsenal, Finsbury Park and Stoke Newington stations, as well as numerous regular bus routes linking the surrounding area.  There are an array of fashionable local amenities, shops, bars and restaurants within just a short distance, with Stoke Newington also close by.  The area is also well served by fantastic schools, including William Patten (rated Ofsted Outstanding), just a five minute walk away.Comments from the owner:What do you love most about your home? I fell in love with this flat more than two decades ago when I viewed it on an unusually hot day in May.  The flat had just been converted and was an empty shell with a garden of mud and weeds.  Once through the door I was immediately struck by how peaceful it was: I could hear the birds singing.  It was also lovely and cool, and like a little house, with large landings and stairs.  I have loved living here, it's so safe and secure that I leave my back door open during the summer.  The garden is wonderful now and we see squirrels, birds and even the odd fox cub.  The flat is cool in the summer and lovely and warm in the winter.  Myself and the other long term owners got together and bought the freehold which we now manage together with everyone looking out for one another.  Why are you moving? I'm moving to Edinburgh to be near my family. What do you love about the area? This is a great area to live in.  We have some great restaurants nearby - Patron and Au Lac being two of my favourites.  Transport is brilliant, Arsenal and Finsbury Park Tube stations are both within a 10 minute walk.  Buses too are fab with the 19 for Upper Street and the West End and the 4 for the Barbican, Tate Modern and The Southbank. Clissord Park is just down the road and if you fancy messing about on the water Finsbury Park and Woodberry Nature Reserve are less than 15 minutes walk away. We also have the Pleasance Theatre, Upper Street, London's Best Comedy Club at Crouch End, and Stoke Newington Church Street all on the door step.  We have great local shops; wine, cheese, books, flowers, you can get everything you need and you will love, as I have, the real sense of community that is here. Mountgrove Road will be part of the upcoming Brownswood Conservation area.Ref: ISL

For any enquiries please call   or alternatively email   This four-bedroom semi-detached home with three reception rooms, three bathrooms/shower rooms, and rarely available off-street parking will be appreciated by families and investors. Located on a quiet residential cul-de-sac, it is only a minutes’ walk from Colliers Wood tube station, a 24-hour Tesco, convenience stores and cafes including Costa Coffee and Co-Op, a petrol station, Colliers Wood recreation ground and Wandle Park, Priory Retail Park (including Aldi and Dunelms), Sainsbury’s, Marks and Spencer, a Post Office, Singlegate Primary School – the list goes on! The property is offered without the need for an onward chain. EXTERIORThere is parking for two/three cars on the block-paved drive, unusual in this residents-permit parking area.  GROUND FLOOR A porch with UPVC sliding door opens to the hall with a wooden panel-glazed door and adjacent stained-glass windows, laminated flooring, double radiator and stairs to the first floor. A lounge/living room with a bay window to the front and double radiator is currently used as a bedroom with space for a three-piece suite and further furniture.  The dining room can hold a dining table for six people, with two built-in cupboards (one fitted with shelves) and a built-in dresser, as well as an inset gas fireplace in steel surround.  The room is finished with laminated wood flooring and spotlights.  Planning permission granted for 6m rear extension, and can accommodate a sofa and other furniture, with a patio door leading to the garden, a solid wood door to the side alley and a panel-glazed door through to the kitchen.  There is a double radiator and laminated wood flooring. The kitchen has laminated worktops to two walls with a breakfast bar for three people, an integrated gas hob and electric oven, space for a fridge/freezer, stainless steel 1.5 sink with mixer tap, space for under-counter washing machine, gas central heating boiler mounted in cupboard, casement windows to the side and rear aspects, a tiled floor, smooth plastered ceiling and recessed lighting.  The rest of the ground floor consists of a store room (which can be utilised as an office/study), ground-floor toilet (with sink), and a separate shower area with Triton electric power shower. FIRST FLOORStairs with open wooden balustrades lead to the first floor landing.  Bedroom 2 can accommodate a King-size bed, has carpet and a bay window to the front with Venetian blinds over, picture rail, smooth plastered ceiling with coving, a fireplace with mantelpiece, double radiator and dimmer-controlled spotlights. Bedroom 3 is a double-bed sized room with a fireplace and built-in corner cupboard with shelves, dimmer-controlled spotlights, and French doors with a transom window and Venetian blinds over. Bedroom 4 is a single bedroom with laminated wood flooring, a radiator, a window to the front with Venetian blinds over, and spotlight fittings.A door leads to an enclosed staircase to the second floor. SECOND FLOORThe stairs open directly into the spacious main bedroom, with room for a super King-size bed, plenty of room for other furniture, and with eaves storage cupboards.  There is laminated wood flooring and recessed lighting, a casement window to the rear aspect with Venetian blinds over, two Velux windows.  The en-suite contains a shower cubicle with thermostatic shower and drench showerhead, dual-flush toilet, heated towel rail, pedestal sink with waterfall mixer tap, tiled walls and floor, smooth plastered ceiling with recessed lighting and an opaque casement window to the rear. GARDEN (Approximately 9.5 metres long excluding shed)Paving slab-laid patio with wooden shed, external water tap, surrounded by full-height close-boarded wooden fencing.

Odhams Walk occupies a prominent position within Covent Garden bordered by Long Acre to the south, Endell Street to the east and Shelton Street to the north, originally designed to create an oasis of calm in the centre of the city, a vision that has proven successful and remains popular to this day. Situated on the first floor podium, this well-proportioned and recently refurbished one bedroom apartment is located in a wonderful private position within the development. The ample living space is well laid out and includes a separate kitchen, good sized reception room, large bedroom and shower room. The apartment also benefits from several large storage cupboards  In 1979, the historic Odham's Print Works was redeveloped by the Greater London Council with guidance from the Covent Garden Community Association to create Odhams Walk, a mixed-use development with residential flats located above a podium of commercial premises and parking. Architect Donald Ball saw the commission as a once in a lifetime opportunity, his design envisaging a public piazza connected by pedestrian ramps to the street, allowing free public access to the complex, with the flats in the two lower levels organised around inner courtyards and open galleries. Odhams Walk in the present day is a prime example of award-winning modernist architecture and successful urban planning, with an active resident's association keen to protect the development's unique position as a village in its own right within Covent Garden.NB: Some internal photos have been digitally enhanced for marketing purposes WHAT WE LOVE  Bright, well proportioned roomsSimple modern architectureOasis of calm in London's West EndMoments from the PiazzaSeven Dials on the door step. WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW  On-site Estate ManagerResident-only access after-hoursRecently refurbishedLong Leasehold - 152 years (approx)Service Charge - £1723 per annum.

A delightful and unique two bedroom ground floor apartment in a handsome Victorian conversion, in the heart of sought after Wanstead. Recently refurbished to a beautiful modern standard, it retains all its original charm and character, and has the little extra luxuries of a utility room and a pantry. Plus, drivers will rejoice in the bonus of an off-street parking spot.IF YOU LIVED HERE… You'll step into your broad hallway and find your utility room on the right. Continue past the built-in storage for your lounge, an impressive 275 square foot of space lit by a classic bay window. Pale painted floorboards lie underfoot, and you've plenty of room for dining. Opposite is your elegant and bright bathroom, with floating vanity sink and dedicated rainfall shower tiled in marbled light grey. Next to that is the eighty five square foot kitchen, with plenty of creamy white units and sage green metro backsplashes. Through the pantry you'll find your way out into your secluded courtyard garden – it's a glorious space bordered by raised beds and perfect for morning coffee. Back inside are your two bedrooms. The first is nearly eighty square feet and would also make for a fine home working space, while your master bedroom's a generous 110 square foot double overlooking the garden with handsome timber floorboards underfoot.You're tucked away moments from the charming village centre of Wanstead, with a pleasant public green and all the shops and eateries you could want. This property has an abundance of light throughout and must be viewed to truly appreciate all that it has to offer.Safeguarding Your InformationImportant information regarding cybercrime. It has come to our attention that computer hackers are seeking to target property transactions to access funds. As we understand it, they will either hack a phone, tablet, or PC, maybe by sending out a random email that mistakenly gets opened, or even whilst opening a website, which gives them access to that machine. The reason for us mentioning this to you is to alert you that once they have gained access, they can monitor email correspondence, without you knowing, throughout a transaction. Then, at a relevant point, e.g. just before the exchange of contracts, they can send an email to a client mirroring that of their solicitor asking for funds to be transferred to a certain bank account. That money then, of course, disappears. Fortunately, we have not had such an instance but, feel it important to highlight the fact that if you do receive an email requesting funds, it is worth checking with the person concerned by phone that it is genuine and the bank details are correct, before sending money. This could, of course, affect other areas of finance beyond the property. OPEN FOR VIEWINGS DURING LOCKDOWN 13 Tennyson Road is a beautiful 3-bedroom terraced home, located in the 'Poets area' of desirable borough of Wimbledon, London. Set on a tranquil street, the property is only moments from Wandle Meadow Nature Park (a 4.15-hectare reserve with a Grade 1 conservation title). The exterior's striking diamond-tiled walkway draws the eye to a characterful front door, whilst a pebbled front yard and 5 sash windows are offset by warm-hued brick work. Inside, an entry hallway leads to the family lounge room featuring attractive floor boards, an original fireplace, and large light-filled windows. Wooden shutters and built-in bookshelves elevate the interior with a timeless elegance. Adjoining the lounge room is a spacious eat-in kitchen, showcasing statement tiles, a gas ring hob, wooden benchtops, and a glass-fronted oven. A multitude of cupboards offer ample space for storing food, whilst a dining table can accommodate 4 to 6 people. Leading on from the kitchen, a glorious conservatory delivers extra entertaining space. Fall in love with its tiled floor, bright vanilla walls, and a stunning glass ceiling. The back garden is easy-to-maintain, distinguished by mature shrubs, pretty ground pebbles, and barbeque decking. A chocolate-box storage shed is perfect for storing tools whilst fences and trellis ensure privacy. On the first floor, two bedrooms impress with their sun-filled windows, neutral walls, and soft carpets, whilst a family bathroom boasts handsome blue panelling and a full tub / shower. A second stairwell leads to the very top of the house, where the third bedroom is located. This charismatic space offers floor-to-ceiling windows and plenty of privacy. The added bonus — its own wet-room style en suite! Within walking distance of Haydons Road and Wimbledon train stations and local amenities including bars, schools and restaurants, 13 Tennyson Road is the ideal property for growing families, investors, and professional couples.

The Development  This is a unique new community that infuses life into a richly historic site. From the 19th century it was a location for one of Bermondsey's many tanneries, and later, it contained premises owned by Crosse and Blackwell who made their famous Branston Pickle here.  London Square has harnessed the talents of masterplan architects to create a vibrant destination that opens up a site long hidden from view.  A collection of one, two and three bedroom apartments, duplexes and townhouses. Between each of the buildings will be 8 new courtyards and garden squares with walkways connecting the development to the surrounding streets, bringing a new lease of life.  Based in Bermondsey since 1993, Tannery Arts, an independent charity making contemporary art accessible to the public will be an integral element of the development. Creating a double height exhibition space, new studios and display cases along Tannery Way. London Square Bermondsey is also incredibly well located. Bermondsey Street is just a short walk away offering a treasure trove of the quirky and individual. Discover Borough Market one of the most exciting places in the city to buy fine food from all over the world.  If your Monday-to-Friday life takes you into the city both More London and London Bridge are easily reachable by bike or on foot. Canary wharf is just two stops east on the Jubilee line from Bermondsey station which is approximately 14 minutes walk away.  It also has its fair share of open space, with the newly regenerated Bermondsey Spa gardens just around the corner or Southwark Park with 61 acres of green space just a 9 minute cycle away. And it's equally well placed for higher education opportunities.  Images depict the Show Home at London Square Bermondsey and are indicative only

Walthamstow is unquestionably one of the most desirable places to live in east London. Keeping community at its heart, Trinity Woolwich will not only build new quality homes, it will become a place where people choose to love, live and grow.  Woolwich offers a whole host of local attractions and amenities to explore; from food markets to museums, Woolwich has something for every taste. For the Foodies, Woolwich is home to a busy food market 6 days a week, a host of cosy cafes selling delicious cakes and pastries and restaurants offering food from all over the world. For those with a passion for real ale, the independent Hop Stuff Brewery is a great place to sit and savour a beer - or two! If shopping is your bag, the vibrant local high street with a mix of independent shops and well-known high street brands, as well as connections to Westfield Stratford, Bluewater and Canary Wharf, offers shopping for even the most discerning buyer.  The Location  With excellent transport links to arguably the most dynamic and exciting city in the world, Woolwich (zone 4) is perfectly placed for those who love the buzz of London and beyond.  ROAD Woolwich is well connected to the national road network via the South Circular (A205) and the A206 (linking to the M25 at Dartford), making it easy to travel further afield.  AIR London City airport is only a 2 stop ride away from Woolwich on the DLR, connecting Woolwich to popular destinations in Europe and beyond.  BOAT Take a different approach to your journey and hop on the Thames Clipper from Woolwich Royal Arsenal (8 mins to the O2 and 29 mins to Canary Wharf) or the famous Woolwich Ferry from Woolwich Pier.  FUTURE CONNECTIONS Woolwich Crossrail is anticipated to open in 2021 and will herald in a new era for Woolwich. With 12 services an hour, passengers will be able to reach Canary Wharf in 8 minutes, Liverpool Street in 14 minutes and Bond Street in just 22 minutes. *The Help to Buy Equity Loan scheme is available on selected properties in England, on homes up to £600,000 (£400,000 in Scotland). Interest will be payable on the equity loan after five years, at an annual gross rate of 1.75%, rising at the RPI plus 1% per year thereafter (except in Scotland). This living room conveniently opens onto the kitchen which is fitted with a range of stylish recently installed high and low level units providing ample storage, with integrated stainless steel appliances, wooden floorboards and built-in wooden breakfast bar/ dining table. There are two double bedrooms both with sunny south west facing windows providing lots of natural light, with built in wardrobes, a good sized bathroom and generous hallway with plenty of space for a bookcase and storage, fitted with attractive wooden floorboards to the hallway.  In recent years the block has had a major, mainly external, refurbishment and also has a lift, entry phone and a parking space, which is included in the sale of the flat and is available on a separate lease.  The property is conveniently located close to excellent transport links into the City and West End, including Stoke Newington Station into Liverpool Street and numerous bus routes.  Transport links at Dalston and Finsbury Park are also a short bus journey away.The vibrant Stoke Newington Church Street with its boutique shops, restaurants, coffee shops and bars is moments away and the open spaces of Clissold Park and Abney Park are within easy walking distance.

description: A superb, recently refurbished loft apartment within the ever-popular Dehavilland Studios, Clapton. The building, which was once an aircraft parts factory, was converted into live/work spaces in the 90's and remains one of the most iconic examples of industrial Art Deco architecture anywhere in the world.  The property is offered in exceptional condition throughout, extends to over 1200 sq/ft including mezzanine space and retains many original factory features including polished concrete double height ceilings, hardwood floors and large factory windows that flood the space with light.  The property is located in Clapton, one of East London most rapidly developing areas. It is a short walk to Millfields Park, River Lea Canals and the Hackney Marshes nature reserves. Nearby sporting facilities include riding stables, ice skating rink and a rowing club.  Dehavilland Studios is located within easy reach of the exceptional choice of boutiques, bars and restaurants available in Stoke Newington, Hackney, nearby Chatsworth Road and the ever-changing Upper Clapton Road.  Transport links include a 0.3 mile walk to Clapton Overground Station which offers direct access to Liverpool Street within 15 mins and numerous bus routes to Stoke Newington Church Street and Manor House Tube Station. The property is sold with share of freehold and an allocated off-street parking space.

The apartment was built to a design by an architect formerly working under the RIBA acclaimed Simon Astridge Architecture Workshop with a private south-west facing garden, designed by Chelsea Flower Show gold-medal winners. Wonderfully positioned at the top of a quiet residential street, Crofton Road is elevated above the nearby neighbourhoods of Camberwell and Peckham. Attention to detail has been the organising principle throughout the apartment; maple joinery, Venetian Terrazzo, tadelakt plaster and an exquisite colour palette are but a few of the elements which set the apartment apart from its peers. Accessed from the street on perpendicular Lyndhurst Grove, a striking façade of marine blue, with contrasting sun mural, alludes to the contemporary alterations that beguile the original fabric. The mural is a popular local landmark, having first been painted in the 1990s by former residents and which has been recently published in the local newspaper; the Peckham peculiar. The reception/ dining room is situated at the front of the building, lit with a large east-facing bay window. The principal bedroom lies in the centre of the plan and has direct access to the garden through beautiful, bespoke, maple framed doors (found throughout). Next to the principal bedroom is the bathroom which is plastered in a dark green tadelakt and a dappled green venetian terrazzo. The kitchen, to the rear of the building, is highlighted by a bespoke triple-width sink with brass hardware and Venetian Terrazzo matching that found in the bathroom. The sink is set into a small extension, also in maple, creating a further bay window, flooding the space with light and brilliantly tying together the design elements of the apartment. To the right of the sink, a rarely installed, albeit essential feature is a plumbed wine tap. Meticulously planned, the kitchen has extensive hidden storage and plywood work surfaces. From the kitchen, the second of the double bedrooms can be found at the rear, opening up through glazed double doors onto the garden. Crofton Road is a quiet residential street which gently inclines above the neighbourhoods of Peckham and Camberwell. The South London Gallery, recently extended and re-designed by 6a Architects, sits at the foot of the hill, beside the campus of UAL: Camberwell. Independent restaurants, bars and cafes are all nearby and include perennial local favourites, Silk Road, Forza Win, Levans, Pedler and Kudu. Larger establishments such as Frank’s rooftop bar, Peckham Levels and the Bussey Building attract crowds from across the city. The green spaces of Telegraph Hill & Peckham Rye Park are a short walk away. Peckham Rye is the nearest station, just a ten-minute walk away, running London Overground services to Shoreditch High Street and Dalston Junction in one direction and Clapham Junction in the other. Connections to the Jubilee Line can be reached at Canada Water (ten minutes) and the Northern Line at Clapham High Street (11 minutes). There are also services to London Bridge (seven minutes) and Blackfriars (11 minutes). Nearby Denmark Hill station has non-stop services to Victoria (nine minutes) and Elephant & Castle (seven minutes).

One Park Drive is a single harmonious building, however its 58 storeys are made up of three typologies, each with its own distinct characteristics.  The central section of the building features the interlocked Cluster apartments. The rhythmic alternating floor plates formed by the mirroring and rotation of apartment plans establishes visual complexity whilst maintaining clarity of internal space. The Cluster apartments integrate all elements from space planning to the material palette in a seamless way, harmonising with the approach to the exterior façade. The natural material selection works with texture and light to capture a calm interior sense and character linked to the external expression of the building. The lower levels are connected with the parks and the water, the higher floors with the sky.  The apartment interiors deliver a complexity and surprise that complements One Park Drive's powerful exterior form, as well as creating a sense of calm and a luxurious intimacy.  The interiors focus on natural materials and finishes throughout with special attention being paid to the tactile surfaces. The three typologies so powerfully expressed in the exterior architecture have their own distinct yet harmonious personalities.  One Park Drive celebrates its communal spaces. Spread across the ground and first floors, they are all fully integrated into the life of the building and positively engage with its surroundings.  The reception, concierge, lounge, library and cinema are all on the ground floor. The first floor houses the exclusive health club including a 20-metre pool, gym, studio space and spa. Dramatic floor-to-ceiling glazing invites stunning views across the surrounding parks, gardens and water.  Canary Wharf Group's careful stewardship of the private Canary Wharf Estate over the past 30 years has made it one of the safest, connected, best maintained and most vibrant districts in the capital. And now the most exciting evolution in the Estate's history has begun - the creation of the new residential district.  It will be a neighbourhood of green spaces and waterside boardwalks. A neighbourhood that's both an oasis of calm and one of the most well connected in Europe. A neighbourhood where it will be just as easy to grab the essentials of daily life as it will be to take in live music or theatre. It will be a local neighbourhood with all the benefits of being part of a private Estate.

address: Queen Of Denmark Court, Greenland Passage, SE16
description: This spacious Danish-built apartment across ground and 1st floors is set directly on the Thames embankment in Greenland Dock’s most prestigious development.   Freshly painted white throughout with simple, easy to clean lines, approached through a scented herb garden, it is the perfect post-Covid home. The large hallway has ample storage for coats and shoes, direct access to a shower room. With ash floors and open curved pine stairs, the vast double aspect reception room is flooded with light from the 30 ft high window wall. This opens onto a balcony with oblique river views and space for café table and chairs. Above, the generous 1st floor galleried reception room with elegant Hülsta lighted wall unit is ideal for entertaining or television. The attractive double bedroom has river views and ensuite, two single bedrooms have juliet balconies. Gallery and single rooms lend themselves to use as offices or home gym. Ample storage throughout. The front door opens directly onto extensive communal gardens, shielded by flowering quinces.  With mature trees, lawn, beautiful flowerbeds, this is literally a private park – all the benefits but no upkeep. Secure garaging plus 2 permit spaces on a private road.SituationLive in Queen of Denmark Court and avoid a crowded commute: travel by boat for work or pleasure. 3-minute walk to Thames Clipper Pier, then 4-minute ride to Canary Wharf opposite.  Close to the Tower of London and London Bridge (1.7 miles,) go by river to Borough Market, South Bank, Tate Modern, Waterloo and more.Fast connections from Canada Water Underground: Jubilee Line to Westminster 10 mins, Bond Street 13 mins.  The Overground into the City 10 mins. or 30 mins by car.  Drive Rotherhithe Tunnel to Canary Wharf 18 mins. (ample parking.)  City Airport 4 miles with 5 transport choices.  St Thomas’ Hospital and Evelina for children at Westminster Bridge.Canada Water Underground is 10 mins. waterside walk, where shopping centre, sophisticated cafés and bars circle new library and theatre on the lake. Greenland Dock combines dockland history with natural beauty and leisure activities: canoe, windsurf or fish; sail from South Dock Marina; visit Russia Dock Woodlands ecological park and playground.  Pubs include the 400 yr old Mayflower in Rotherhithe Village.  Greenwich is 6 mins. on the Thames Clipper or a 2-mile riverbank cycle ride. Wander the Royal Park, Observatory, Cutty Sark, intriguing markets and shops. The O2 is one further stop.

Perfectly complementing this lovely apartment are two sizeable storage cupboards which see located in the hallway.  King Henry Lodge is a stylish development of 32 one and two bedroom retirement apartments in the suburban London district of Chingford, bordered by Epping Forest, the River Lea and King George’s Reservoir. Chingford is home to a number of listed buildings and landmarks including Queen Elizabeth’s Hunting Lodge, dating back to 1543. Epping Forest is London’s largest open space and a great place to escape the hustle and bustle of the city and enjoy the beautiful landscape and wildlife. The lodge is in a great location close to the local shops, cafes, doctors and pharmacy. Transport is excellent with a bus stop almost outside with regular trips into central London and even a night bus service from Trafalgar Square.  King Henry Lodge has been designed with safety and security at the forefront, Apartment 18 has an emergency Careline system installed, monitored by the onsite Lodge Manager during the day and 24 hours, 365 days a year by the Careline team. Careline integrated intruder alarm, secure video entry system and sophisticated fire and smoke detection systems throughout both the apartment and communal areas provide unrivalled peace of mind.  King Henry Lodge’s manager, Robert, is on hand throughout the day to support the residents and keep the development in perfect shape. Robert arranges many regular events in the Owners’ Lounge from coffee mornings to games afternoons. For any visiting guests, the furnished twin guest suite is perfect and available for just £15 per night for 1 visitor and £20 (£10 each) for 2 visitors. The same excellent value is available across more than 100 Churchill Retirement Lodges for owners to visit throughout the UK.  King Henry Lodge is managed by the award winning Millstream Management Services, working closely with Churchill Retirement Living and Stratton & King to maintain the highest standards of maintenance and service for every lodge and owner.  King Henry Lodge requires at least one apartment resident to be over the age of 60 with any second resident over the age of 55. Presented with a myriad of features from a by gone era, this is one property not to be missed.Positioned just moments from the award winning Downhills Park and the lordship Rec, a short walk to West Green Road, bars, eateries and of course Seven sisters underground station (Victoria Line zone 3) this home is most certainly not running with the crowd.Every consideration has been made to keep one eye on modernity without losing the most important aspect of Victorian charm. The working fireplace sets the tone beautifully. Neutral coloured walls, cast iron fireplace with ornate tiling inset and contrasting engineered wooden floors encapsulate everything that is great about converted period homes. From there we move on to the double bedroom. Again light coloured walls and wooden flooring with double doors leading on to the garden. The Bathroom is well presented and laid out to maximise space. There is natural light, a must for those who enjoy hot showers in the mornings and ventilation therefore, is a must. White walls, tiled surrounds and shelving ensure all Bathroom needs are met. The kitchen has a range of white wall and base units with a beech coloured worksurface. Again thoughtful use of shelving in addition to the cupboards makes storing cook books and other items a cinch. There is a window and door to the side giving access to the all important outdoor space that is so coveted at the moment. The second bedroom although smaller than the main, has a wonderful view of the garden and is the perfect room for those with a child, as is the case here. The Garden is gorgeous. lawn to the centre with a mature tree lending plenty of character, shade and entertainment as is evident here by the child swing attached. The back boundary is brick built and has a raised hardstanding. Currently used as a seating area, the Garden is large enough to consider this space for a work office or perhaps a studio for those with an artistic flair. Mature in presentation, this is a place to enjoy when weather permits! If this sounds like your next home, call now to schedule an appointment.

Vacant- ready for rapid exchange of contracts to safeguard the £15,000 stamp duty discount  Riviera House on Middle St, EC1, is an exclusive collection of 10 high specification residences, contained within a distinguished 1890's warehouse building. The whole building has just been fully redeveloped to the highest standards. A cleverly designed blend of period features and contemporary finishes, it offers sophisticated living in a stylish hi-tech space. It features 2 luxury double bedrooms with extensive bespoke fitted wardrobe space, a fully fitted kitchen with Neff cooking appliances and black granite worktops. Parking is available to residents close by currently at £800 per year. Designed with all the latest hi-tech gadgets, home automation, and innovative features. Middle St is a hidden gem discretely tucked away in an exceptionally quiet historic conservation area, close by are St Bartholomew the Great church (the oldest in London) the William Wallace memorial (William was executed here) and the "Golden boy at Pye corner" ( marking where the fire of London ended ). Opposite are listed Victorian period buildings.  Located in the "City of London" borough, within the zone 1 travel zone, council tax is low and refuse is collected every weekday. Extensive shopping on Cheapside is 5 mins away as are groceries from nearby Waitrose and several Tesco's ..There are numerous first-class restaurants including Michelin starred Club Gascon and the new "Asia Ivy". Smithfield Rotunda garden and the Charterhouse Square are green areas to be enjoyed. Virgin active gym is only 2 minutes walk.  Transport links are unrivaled with 4 underground stations nearby, Barbican only 2 minutes walk with St Pauls, Farringdon and Blackfriars 5 minutes walk. The new Cross Rail Farringdon station is 2 minutes away. Numerous buses pass close by. Council tax band D currently £973 p.a. Low service charges currently £950 p.a.

A wonderful house, positioned in arguably the finest location of SE1.This beautiful period house includes a number of the original features such as wood panelling, wooden stairs and fireplaces. The proportions are superb with THREE double bedrooms and a large bathroom upstairs. In addition there is also a separate cloakroom for added convenience. On the ground floor you can find an impressive 25ft long double reception room, complete with a good size kitchen and a sun room which is adjacent to the kitchen creating the opportunity to knock into one room, subject to the usual planning consents.There is a huge benefit off-road parking included with the house which is incredibly rare to find in Central London, which can be located to the rear of the wonderful south facing rear garden, that boasts its very own grape vine. The garden offers extra width than most in the street, and also has the added advantage of being totally private and not overlooked.There is also a basement measuring just shy of 200 sq ft which comes with wine racks, and is perfect for storage, or more. (subject to planning)The area is surprisingly quiet given its location. There is hardly any traffic as Roupell Street is a no-through road. Residents’ street parking is plentiful.You may be able to guess by looking at the facade, but this house wasn’t always a residential home. It was formally a florist that went by the name of Phoebie's before being turned in a residential house in more recent years. The house has been a happy family home for the family that lived here for many years. This is in no small part due to the fact that the residents in this enclave of Georgian houses close to Waterloo and Southwark stations form such a friendly, close-knit community. There are regular neighbourhood events (funded by proceeds from the TV and film location work), including Christmas and summer street parties. Did you know that the Grade II-listed houses are regularly used as a backdrop for films, TV programmes and photoshoots. You may recognise them from programmes such as Call the Midwife, Doctor Who, Legend, and the opening credits of Ant and Dec’s Saturday Night Takeaway.Another recent event celebrated the 20th anniversary of Konditor & Cook, the legendary bakery founded by a local resident that adorns the corner of Cornwall Road closest to Waterloo station. Their irresistible cakes are popular with locals and commuters alike.The house forms part of the coveted Roupell Street Conservation Area, which contains some of the best-preserved Georgian terraces in London.Roupell Street is one of four streets developed between the 1820s and 1840s. The houses were built by local businessman John Roupell, who owned a lead smelting and scrap metal business in Bear Lane, Southwark. In all, John and his son Richard built 82 houses in four streets. Roupell Street still bears the family name but the others have been renamed.Roupell Street was built as an artisan’s cottage in the early 1800s and forms part of one of London’s best-preserved Georgian terraces.The Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre and BFI Imax, and off course the glorious South Bank and River Thames are all within 0.5 miles away from the house, further highlighting the unrivalled location of this home. A vast selection of pubs, bars, restaurant, shops and other amenities can equally be found within striking distance.Transport links are the best you can find. With Waterloo underground and over ground stations just 0.2 miles away, meaning getting around the Capital and the South of England is easy. Many residents prefer to walk to their destinations such as the West End and the City which can be achieved with ease.

description: Keating Estates are proud to bring to market this beautiful three double bedroom apartment, boasting a roof terrace spanning the length of the property, in a secure development on Upper Tulse Hill.Full Description - Vaulted ceilings and beaming skylights are a running theme throughout this bright and airy three double bedroom apartment. The circular flow and multiple access points onto the terrace, really add to the light and airy atmosphere throughout this apartment.The master bedroom sits opposite the second set of doors to the terrace, benefitting from light at either end of the room and the two further bedrooms sit adjacent to the main bedroom. The third bedroom is currently set up as a home office and walk-in wardrobe but has been used as a double bedroom in the past. The three bedrooms share a contemporary three piece bathroom. Upper Tulse Hill is the perfect spot to escape from the hustle and bustle of city life and this apartment has all the benefits that are associated with smaller modern build developments, nestled away quietly overlooking the surrounding area. This tranquil space is the ideal property for a range of potential purchasers, from first-time buyers and anyone looking for a conveniently located pied a terre with next to no ongoing maintenance, to buy-to-let purchasers or anyone looking to upsize from a smaller property. Three first floor bedrooms and a family bathroom plus two further bedrooms and and a shower room on the second floor.The ground floor of the house has a wonderful sense of scale, with a large double living room at the front of the plan and a bright kitchen / dining room that opens to the South-East facing garden at the rear.The living room retains its elegant original proportions, with a west-facing bay window and a pair of fireplaces. Stripped original floorboards run underfoot and House of Hackney adorns the walls. A cleverly conceived laundry room provides excellent storage while also creating a sense of division. The kitchen is incredibly bright as light floods in from the skylights above throughout the day. Diespeker & Co Terrazo worktops, a striking orange Vola tap and soft pink units bring a contemporary aesthetic to the period interior. Bi-folding doors open onto the private garden.The first floor of the house retains its original layout, with three bedrooms and a family bathroom finished with tiles from Bert & May, underfloor heating, a rainshower and huge family bath. The master bedroom has bespoke shutters and wardrobes that are set either side of the tiled fireplace. On the top floor are two further bedrooms, and a bathroom with a wonderful concrete sink by Kast.Leythe Road is a five-minute walk from South Action station for Overground services, and a ten-minute walk from Acton Town Underground station on the Piccadilly line. It is a short bus ride to the District Line from both Turnham Green and Chiswick Park stations. There are also good road links out of town via the M4.The house is firmly in the catchment for the incredibly popular Ofsted-rated ‘Outstanding’ Ark Priory Primary School and its sister school Ark Byron. As well as Twyford School, the soon to be opened Ark Soane secondary and Chiswick High School. In the private sector local parents choose Orchard House and Ravenscourt Park Prep for Primary and Latymer and St Pauls for senior.There are plenty of open green spaces nearby. South Acton Park is local, and is a short walk to Turnham Green while the green spaces of Kew Gardens and Chiswick House and Gardens that lead down towards the river and Thames Path are also within easy reach.

description: This exceptional, architect-designed 2/3 bedroom, split-level garden flat with off street parking effortlessly blends contemporary interiors with period features. This one-of-a-kind flat is no ordinary period conversion. It has undergone a radical transformation creating flexible and practical living spaces and, with architectural flourishes inside and out, it has a sense of playfulness. The heart of this home is the bespoke kitchen-diner with large Crittal-style doors that open out to a private rear garden. Timber slats on the wall and ceiling add drama and interest to the dining area. The bespoke kitchen includes a Proboil boiling water tap and a Neff gas hob and electric oven. The rest of the ground floor is made up of a reception room at the front (which could double as a bedroom), a reading room behind this with a picture window overlooking the kitchen, a stylish bathroom (with underfloor heating) and a double bedroom at the back. The lower ground floor has been turned into a guest room (with built-in wardrobes and a dressing table) along with a shower room and a cupboard with plumbing for a washing machine. From this level the large cellar can be accessed. The flat was fully re-wired in 2016 providing an extensive range of sockets (including USB plugs) and 4 Dali Phantom built-in ceiling speakers. A 'Nuaire' fresh air ventilation system covers the basement, kitchen and 2nd reception room, and there is underfloor heating with a separate thermostat in the kitchen-diner. The garden provides a fabulous entertaining space with a barbeque and fire pit area with LED lights. Beyond this is a lawn and, at the back, the bonus of an insulated home office with power and lighting.Transport links into town could hardly be easier. Brockley station is a short walk away from where there are fast and frequent services into London Bridge in around 10 mins. In addition, you can pick up the London Overground towards Shoreditch and Highbury and Islington meaning that Canary Wharf is a mere 30-minute commute changing at Canada Water. Crofton Park station is also within easy reach for Thameslink service into Blackfriars and St Pancras.For evening entertainment, there's no shortage of bars and restaurants to choose from. A few places of note include The Gantry (a rustic, French-style restaurant), the Orchard (a friendly restaurant with a wine bar) and Brockley's Rock (an award-winning fish and chip shop). Coffee shops are plentiful including Browns and The Broca. You can pick up groceries from various supermarkets nearby.As for green spaces, Hilly Fields is close by where there is a children's playground, café, enclosed picnic area, cricket pitch and 3 tennis courts. On Saturdays, pop along to the award-winning Brockley Market on Lewisham Way for fresh groceries and a bite to eat.

A beautifully enhanced, enormous maisonette with 3-bedrooms, a kitchen/reception, bathroom, en suite shower and your own front door. Close to good schools and beautiful parks. Share of freehold.Not only is this as large as many nearby houses, it also has its own front door. The flat is split over the first and second floors, the latter being a nicely finished loft conversion. Starting at the top, the principal bedroom has a ply panel floor, handmade floating shelves, and a built-in desk with dormer views across to Canary Wharf. The en suite has a shower with an oversized rainwater rose, a back-to-wall W.C, and a countertop basin above cabinets. The main living space is a huge room, stretching over 26 feet long. Cream kitchen units are fitted and there’s a stainless-steel dishwasher, a built-in over and an inset hob. The floor is cork, cabinets and floating shelves have been added at every opportunity and a window seat has been created at the front bay. The smallest bedroom is a roomy single, being 12 feet long. At the rear is a double bedroom with a bright double aspect.The nearest primary school is (much-loved) Ivydale, while older kids can walk to either Harris academy at Peckham Rye (both are rated 'outstanding' by Ofsted).  Although you can walk to three stations in 15/20-minutes (Honor Oak, Nunhead and Brockley), most prefer to jump on a bus to Peckham Rye, the biggest station nearby. You'll find easy connections to London Bridge, Victoria, St Pancras and London Overground services (Clapham Junction, Canada Water, Shoreditch, Hoxton and Dalston Junction). Nunhead station is a short bus ride/20-minute walk away, and one-stop from Peckham Rye station.  Walk across beautiful Peckham Rye Park for bars, shops and restaurants in East Dulwich. Nunhead’s high street has some superb independents including a zero-waste store, craft beer bar, florist, bicycle repair shop and gift shops. Your local boozer is The Ivy House (London’s first co-operatively-owned pub and a really vibrant venue/community asset with plenty to entice all ages), while several excellent pubs fringe Peckham Rye – we love Watson’s General Telegraph, The Herne, and The Rye. Find shops, yoga studios, a multi-screen cinema and buzzing creative scene in nearby Peckham. Nearby, One Tree Hill is a woodland and nature reserve with amazing views across London while Nunhead Cemetery is one of London’s Magnificent Seven cemeteries and very, very beautiful.

The PropertyA unique and stunning, spacious property in a 1,600 sq. ft. tower within the Royal Victoria Patriotic Building in Wandsworth Common. A once in a lifetime opportunity to purchase both a piece of London history and an unusual multi-level residential property. Entering on the second floor you open into a bright and welcoming reception space with stylish fitted kitchen with breakfast bar. and bathroom to the left, a wonderful double bedroom to your right and mezzanine living room in front of you. From here there is a staircase to a galleried area currently used as an office and leading onto a second, bright double bedroom. From the office gallery, there is access to a further staircase, also with its own access to the stairs outside the flat, leading up into a large open plan kitchen and living room within the beams of the tower, before going up again to a raised sleeping area and tiled shower bathroom, and up again to the very top of the turret with 360 degree views of Wandsworth Common and the surrounding area.  There is a designated off-street parking space, and visitor parking is available.The building as a whole has served several functions throughout it's prominent London history and is in the North of Wandsworth Common close to Northcote Road and also the Toast Rack with its pub, cafe and local shops. The building has a residents' courtyard, the fantastic Le Gothique restaurant is on site, as are various artists studios and a small performing arts school providing a lively arts community on your doorstep. Clapham Junction Station is within walking distance, as is Wandsworth Common station and central Wandsworth is nearby.  Disclaimer for virtual viewingsSome or all information pertaining to this property may have been provided solely by the vendor, and although we always make every effort to verify the information provided to us, we strongly advise you to make further enquiries before continuing.If you book a viewing or make an offer on a property that has had its valuation conducted virtually, you are doing so under the knowledge that this information may have been provided solely by the vendor, and that we may not have been able to access the premises to confirm the information or test any equipment. Large One Bed Georgian Gem With Shared Garden on Best Street - CHAIN FREE.Get a taste of period living in this first floor apartment based in a five storey converted Georgian house on sedate Camberwell Grove. The property comes with a share of the communal garden and is offered chain free.The entrance to the apartment is set back from the road by a grassed front garden with a high hedge, keeping it very private. The communal entrance hall has all the period detail as you step up one flight of stairs to find the apartment on the first floor. A small entrance hall greets you with a handy guest loo to the left and a huge storage cupboard with plumbing for the washing machine directly ahead. There are even more cupboards above. Turn right into the huge living area with an open-plan kitchen and lounge/diner to the front and a lovely bedroom to the rear. The ceilings are wonderfully tall and you'll just love that original cornicing. The main reception room is light and airy with three sets of French doors opening out onto a black iron balcony running the width of the apartment. The kitchen is found on the left hand side with space for all your mod cons and some lovely stainless steel counters.  A beautiful period fireplace, flanked by an abundance of shelves, adds the final flourish to this lovely room. Floor to ceiling original doors lead you to the bedroom, also with high ceilings and tall original windows. From here you can reach the adorable bathroom with roll-top bath and its tall sash window overlooking the communal garden.Ruskin Park is a short hop and you have the Butterfly Tennis Club on your doorstep. Enter the property at ground level and into a spacious double reception room. The property has been recently renovated and is in great condition. The reception room benefits from solid wooden flooring throughout and two period fireplaces, making for wonderful features. The room is filled with natural light with an ornate sash window to the front and French windows to the rear, providing a dual aspect and access out to the sizeable garden. The kitchen is a fabulous space filled with natural light and fitted units run the length of the room on both sides, with quartz worktops above. The kitchen comprises a double sink with mixer tap, fitted fridge freezer, dishwasher, fan oven and a four-burner gas hob with extractor above. There is also space for an under-counter washing machine and a separate dryer. At the far end of the room is space for a dining table and chairs to seat two, if one so desires. Continue upstairs to the first floor onto the landing, at the front of the house you will find the master bedroom with two sash windows with plantation shutters overlooking the picturesque square below. The room benefits from three fitted wardrobes and ample space for a king-size bed and bedside tables. The bathroom has been stylishly finished with white metro tiling and contains a bath with shower above, wash basin with mirror above, heated towel rail and a W.C.Upstairs at the rear of the property is a smaller bedroom, still with space for a double and fitted storage. A beautifully presented period flat set over three floors of a mid-terraced period home in the heart of Vauxhall. Enter the property up a private staircase. Directly, at the rear of the property is the kitchen. The galley style kitchen has an abundance of worktop and storage space in the form of white units. There are integrated appliances and a sash window to the rear.  The downstairs WC/utility room accommodates an integrated washing machine, dryer, fitted cupboards as well as a Porter bathroom cabinet and Fired Earth fittings. Going up a further half flight of stairs, you will find the open plan reception and dining rooms. Restored oak wooden flooring runs between both rooms. The dining room to the rear has ample space for large table to seat at least eight. The reception area to the front is presented beautifully, there are three sash windows enjoying views of the street, a charming Real Flame working gas fireplace, subtle cornicing and John Cullen lighting throughout.  The floor above is where the master suite is situated. The bedroom, which is like the reception room below, has sash windows to the front and views of the street. There is ample space for a large double bed, bedside tables and fitted wardrobes either side of the chimney breast. The en-suite bathroom is vast. There is a lovely basin unit, walk-in shower and WC, again benefitting from Fired Earth fittings.  In the converted loft space at the top of the property there is further double bedroom with en-suite bathroom. The bedroom is carpeted and has space for a large double bed and desk area. The bathroom benefits from a bath with overhead shower, WC and a basin. The flat has been modelled and maintained beautifully by the current owner and would represent a wonderful home for any prospective purchasers. The new Nine Elms underground station is approximately 200m away (Northern Line – opening 2021). As an alternative method of transport, there are also Santander Cycle Docking Stations close by.

A three-storey house—complete with garden and one-car driveway—smack in the East End? You may sooner believe in unicorns, but here it is, and it's a little bit magic, too. Somehow meticulously funky, this home weaves together a '70s vibe with clean lines and beautiful joinery to create a space that feels creative and cool, yet warm and welcoming all the same. Past the front lounge, flanked by bespoke ply joinery, is a brilliantly designed open-plan kitchen that's part modern, part retro, with fresh white cabinetry offset by Mexican tiles and a smart, full-wall pegboard. Up the glorious wooden staircase is a larger lounge and third bedroom (currently used as a home office), perfectly balancing communal and private space. Up again are two quiet bedrooms with a tropical-themed tiled bathroom between them and bespoke wardrobes that run the entire length of the top floor. So close to so much, the house is situated in a quiet warren between Hackney and Spitalfields, with Bethnal Green just to the east. That means all three neighbourhoods are walkable, so it's easy to take advantage of all the food and fun on foot. Transport links are plentiful, too, with Overground, Tube and Liverpool Street stations nearby. But the real appeal is your very own house in the beating heart of London.The Owners Say - The street is like a hidden oasis off Brick Lane—a whole house on the boundary of the City. Crosstown Doughnuts is on the doorstep, as is the Original Beigel Shop—pronounced Buy-gul not Bay-gel. (There’s also a Weight Watchers at the church on St. Mathews Row, just in case.)Everything you need is a short walk away, including Bethnal Green and Liverpool Street stations, Shoreditch High Street Overground, the V&A Museum of Childhood, The Geoffrey Museum, Brick Lane Market, Spitalfields Market, Columbia Road Flower Market, York Hall Spa & Swimming pool…I could go on, but I might change my mind about the move!Points To Consider - The EPC gives a current Energy Efficiency Rating of C. The south-facing open living space is awash in sun all day, and that sense of airy lightness follows you throughout the flat—from the freshly remodelled bath with its classic fixtures to the quiet back bedroom.
