description: A truly magnificent period conversion which has been extensively refurbished throughout to create this stunning one of a kind home. DescriptionA stunning chapel conversion which offers approximately 1,245 sq ft of well planned living space, spread across two floors. The current owners have spent considerable time and effort, carefully thinking out every last detail to the renovation, and have spent vast amounts of money using the finest materials available to create this amazing home. There is digital coded secure entry, which then leads you into a large hallway which offers various integrated storage units. This then leads into a generous open plan living area which is flooded with natural light from the substantial feature window. The downstairs offers a versatile living arrangement, and can be customised to suit your personal needs, and the current set up offers a sliding door create a third bedroom if required, or it can remain open which would be ideal for when you wish to entertain friends. There are polished concrete floors, incredibly high vaulted ceilings, and a feature staircase with glass balustrade which leads to the first floor. Therefor, the house is hard wired to take any home automation platform on the market. There is also an advanced heating / cooling system, which can be controlled remotely.LocationWest India DLR is located within just 0.3 miles, offering swift access into the City. Canary Wharf is located just 0.5 miles, offering  access to a variety of local shops, bars and restaurants. description: A light filled 2 bedroom freehold house on a quiet residential street  in the heart of Bethnal Green.  The property sits on a bright east/west axis and offers purchasers the opportunity to create a home to their preferred style, with the  potential to extend (subject to the necessary permissions). ACCOMMODATION & AMENITIES2 Double BedroomsLiving RoomConservatorySeparate KitchenFront and Rear GardensShower RoomAllocated Parking SpaceAccommodation comprises a spacious living room with double doors that lead onto a conservatory, separate  kitchen, attractive front and rear gardens with the rear garden boasting a sunny west aspect. The property sits within a peaceful cul-de-sac and also benefits from an outside storage cupboard and a designated parking space immediately in front of the house.   The house is fully double glazed and has wooden flooring in the living area and bedrooms. This home has been well maintained by the current owner who has occupied the house for over 25 years and really enjoyed this community feel of this quiet cul-de-sac. Keating Estates are proud to bring to market a handsome example of a four double bedroom Victorian terraced house that brings an exciting and rare opportunity to stamp your own mark on a blank canvas that is also rich in character and ideally located.Full Description - A classic example of a four double bedroom Victorian house, this property on leafy Elms Crescent boasts over 2,000 square feet of internal living space and offers a great opportunity for a wide range of purchasers. Located within easy reach of Abbeville Village, Clapham High Street and Clapham South, this stunning terraced house will appeal to people contemplating moving to this area. With a varied array of cafes, restaurants, shops and transport links on the doorstep, purchasers will be spoiled for choice.The house retains many original features enhanced by stylish additions such as the double-height ceiling at the entrance to the kitchen/diner designed by the previous architect owner. There is nonetheless an opportunity to stamp your own mark on the property. The ground floor comprises a wonderfully bright and airy double-aspect reception, flooded with natural light thanks to the large bay window at the front and original patio door opening onto the garden at the back – both rooms with original fireplaces and beautifully corniced ceilings. Via the double height windowed “void” the hallway opens onto a spacious dining area and kitchen with ample storage and worktop space. French double doors create a seamless transition from inside to outside where purchasers will enjoy relaxing in the sunny and peaceful rear garden, which is over fifty feet in length. The private mature garden with lawn and flowerbeds on either side has a vine covered gazebo at the end for outdoor dining.On the two upper floors are the four double bedrooms. The generous master bedroom runs the width of the property with a large bay window that allows natural light to flood into the room. The second and third double bedrooms overlook the private garden, sharing a family bathroom on the first floor. The bedroom on the second floor is accompanied by a second bathroom with shower. Stairs lead to an additional substantial loft room. Subject to the necessary permissions, re-designing this space and adding a side extension to the kitchen give the opportunity to add space and value to the existing layout. This is an elegant home in an ideal location: a beautiful terrace on a sought-after street - close to the heart of Abbeville Village yet far enough away to retain a real sense of calm. The road is popular for its proximity to the  shops,  restaurants and cafes on Abbeville Road as well as Clapham South and Clapham High Street. Brixton is also within walking distance offering a huge range of shops, restaurants and amenities as well as quick access to the West End on the Victoria Line. Freehold and chain-free.

Going into the flat through a private door off the grand communal entrance, there is a hallway, that leads onto the rooms. There is a bright, fitted kitchen, with a built-in breakfast bar by the window and folding doors opening on to the living room which overlooks a large and inviting residents' gardens. The double size bedroom benefits from a fitted wardrobe and a large window and there is a modern bathroom with shower over the bath. Outside there is free off street parking, with great walking, jogging or cycling along the Thames Path. Deptford's vibrant Market Yard is next to the mainline station and Surrey Quays (Overground) are within a mile. Canada Water Station (Jubilee Line) and Greenwich Village (+DLR) are a short bus ride while Greenland Pier for the Thames riverboat to Canary Wharf and beyond is ten minutes walk along the river. What The Seller Says - "We love the sense of space and tranquillity in the flat created by the high ceilings and majestic Georgian windows and its location by the river Thames. Bedroom and living room look out onto the extensive communal gardens (a lifesaver during lockdown), which are also a haven for wildlife. For exercise there is the Thames Path at our door, and we love walking or cycling by the river."

A stylish, modern one bedroom flat with balcony overlooking Wandsworth Common. This bright and airy flat is on the third floor of a popular purpose built building with lifts and benefits from leafy views of Wandsworth Common. The accommodation comprises: a spacious living room with dual aspect windows which floods the room with light, a separate fully-fitted kitchen, a large double bedroom with built in wardrobe, modern bathroom with shower over bath, spacious balcony, good storage and off street parking. The property is well connected via the transport links at Clapham Junction and Wandsworth Common stations (BR).

This smart one-bedroom apartment is perched on the top floor (4th floor walk-up) of a small period building in the heart of Fitzrovia. Features include good ceiling height, wooden flooring, views towards the BT Tower, and plenty of natural light.Hanson Street is close to the multifarious delights of the Great Titchfield Street Coffee Quarter and just a short stroll from the greenery of The Regents Park. For connectivity, Great Portland Street, Oxford Circus and Goodge Street Stations are all nearby.Having been successfully let for a number of years, this property presents the ideal opportunity to get on the West End property ladder.

address: Ickburgh Road, Hackney Downs, London, E5
description: Comments from the owner:What do you love most about your home? Sitting at the front in the sun watching the world go by; winter honeysuckle as you come up the road;  the first time I ever stepped through the door of this house, I loved the light from the top of the house to the bottom of the hall and the elegant sweep of the staircase. The house has many original features that reflect it's traditional heritage and history, which we have upgraded and sympathetically added to.. Our spacious kitchen dining room is the hub of the household where we spend hours with friends and family eating and sorting out the world, around the fire. There is lots of flexible space with office possibilities, as we now work from home, including potential basement rental income. Finally, we love our fantastic landscaped garden, perfect for breakfast, with the backdrop of trees, giving a sense of green and calm in an otherwise lively and busy environment. It has been a fantastic home for our children to grow up in.Why are you moving? With reluctance, because we love this house and the area but as our children begin to move away to independence, we need to move out of London to give support to our parents.What do you love about the area? Neighbours are reliable and supportive and fun. Diversity and friendliness of the street. People really help each other out. Brilliant transport,  trains/buses, and easy bike access to the canal. , BLOK gym, independent cafes, restaurants, and other shops of interest, records,  microbrewery, books, and bikes. Springfield Park and its arboretum which rolls down to the vibrant canal. Kingfishers, herons, swans, ducks, and much more thrive there; the boat club and Walthamstow wetlands beyond;  giving a connection with the countryside; access to the Olympic Park with excellent sports facilities and the opportunity to walk to a range of international sporting events, and to swim and canoe in the reservoir. Close to Mare Street with a very good theatre and two great small music venues. Ref: ISL

We are pleased to offer for sale this spacious 3 bed apartment located on the 3rd floor of Park West, a period, portered block located close to Marble Arch and all Edgware Road amenities. The flat benefits from 3 bedrooms, 3 bathrooms and a second kitchen area which could be easily used as a study, utility room or dressing area. The reception area is spacious and benefits from hard wood flooring. Modern kitchen and bathrooms.

address: St. Lukes Church, Mayfield Road, N8
description: A wonderful opportunity to purchase a unique, one-bedroom, split-level apartment set within an imposing converted church with immense character and charm. Positioned on a much-favoured residential turning close to the green open space of Stationers park.  Offered on a chain free basis.  The property has been well-maintained by the current owners, benefiting from spacious and tastefully decorated rooms throughout. Further comprising bespoke kitchen/reception, family bathroom, storage space, secluded own private terrace and allocated parking.  The property is conveniently positioned within walking distance to Crouch End’s historic Broadway with its array of amenities and is a short bus ride to Finsbury Park Underground station and Harringay British Rail with links to the City (Moorgate), West End and beyond.

The Development  This is a unique new community that infuses life into a richly historic site. From the 19th century it was a location for one of Bermondsey's many tanneries, and later, it contained premises owned by Crosse and Blackwell who made their famous Branston Pickle here.  London Square has harnessed the talents of masterplan architects to create a vibrant destination that opens up a site long hidden from view.  A collection of one, two and three bedroom apartments, duplexes and townhouses. Between each of the buildings will be 8 new courtyards and garden squares with walkways connecting the development to the surrounding streets, bringing a new lease of life.  Based in Bermondsey since 1993, Tannery Arts, an independent charity making contemporary art accessible to the public will be an integral element of the development. Creating a double height exhibition space, new studios and display cases along Tannery Way. London Square Bermondsey is also incredibly well located. Bermondsey Street is just a short walk away offering a treasure trove of the quirky and individual. Discover Borough Market one of the most exciting places in the city to buy fine food from all over the world.  If your Monday-to-Friday life takes you into the city both More London and London Bridge are easily reachable by bike or on foot. Canary wharf is just two stops east on the Jubilee line from Bermondsey station which is approximately 14 minutes walk away.  It also has its fair share of open space, with the newly regenerated Bermondsey Spa gardens just around the corner or Southwark Park with 61 acres of green space just a 9 minute cycle away. address: Johnson Lodge, Maida Vale, London, W9
description: This fantastic 1 bedroom flat which is situated on the third floor of a modern apartment building offers fantastic living space, private balcony with river views and onsite concierge.Johnson Lodge offers a great location close to a range of local shops that can be found in Maida Vale while lovely canal walks are only a short distance away.

A superb three/four double bedroom townhouse located in Custom House spanning over three floors, located just a short distance from the Asian business park redevelopment, the ExCel Centre, the green open spaces of King George V Park and some sought after schooling. This home comprises of a fitted kitchen/dining room, a downstairs WC, a first floor living room, three good sized bedrooms, a family bathroom and a garage. There is potential for further improvement and extension, therefore creating an ideal family home or investment. No chain.
